title: [YouTube] Fix 403 error and throttling
body: Decrypting `signatureCipher` to fix the recent 403 error, as well as to solve the long-standing download speed throttling issue (#2950). This was made possible thanks to the lightweight JavaScript interpreter [dukpy](https://pypi.org/project/dukpy/).

step_plan.summary: Introduce an optional dukpy-based deciphering path for YouTube's signatureCipher to address 403 errors and throttling, while preserving the existing Python-based deciphering as a fallback. The change is scoped to the YouTube extractor and is controlled by an opt-in flag with dukpy treated as an optional dependency. Modified functions are YouTube.s_to_sig, YouTube.prepare, and YouTube.extract.

steps.operation: Add optional dukpy-based deciphering path and opt-in configuration
steps.reason: Introduce an optional path that uses dukpy to execute YouTube's decipher script for signatureCipher, controlled by a runtime/config flag (e.g., YOU_GET_USE_DUKPY). This preserves the existing Python-based deciphering as a robust fallback when dukpy is unavailable or fails.
steps.side_effects: Adds a new optional dependency (dukpy) and a feature flag. If dukpy is not installed or errors, the code should gracefully fall back to the existing s_to_sig Python path. May require updates to tests to cover both paths and ensure backward compatibility.

steps.operation: Wire in opt-in dukpy path to the prepare stage and propagate decipher path choice
steps.reason: Ensure stream_list construction and signatureCipher detection routes respect the chosen decipher path (dukp vs Python). Propagate the selection flag through to the extraction step so final URLs use the correctly deciphered signature.
steps.side_effects: Tests must simulate both paths; existing flows should remain unchanged when dukpy is not used. Potential minor changes to internal state management to track which path is active.

steps.operation: Ensure final URL construction uses the deciphered signature and add test hooks for both paths
steps.reason: Finalize the URL assembly using the deciphered signature from whichever path was selected. Maintain existing behavior for non-signatureCipher flows. Add tests to verify that both the dukpy-based and Python-based deciphering yield identical final outputs for known samples and that regression tests pass when dukpy is absent.
steps.side_effects: Tests should cover both deciphering paths and common edge cases (non-sigCipher paths, DASH, captions). Potential API/behavior changes if the final URL assembly relies on path-specific data structures; ensure compatibility and document optional dependency behavior.
